research.note: New feature — needs full research phase. Scope: import training plans from external sources (CSV, JSON, PDF, OCR photos, share extension, paste-to-parse). Key unknowns: (1) which sources to support first, (2) exercise name mapping strategy to FitMe's 87-exercise library, (3) how to handle progression rules (linear, wave, percentage-based), (4) whether imported plans replace or supplement the default 6-day PPL split. Related: /research feature import-training-plan, /ux research, competitive analysis of Hevy/Strong/Fitbod import UX.

ux_or_integration.note: New UI surface — Phase 3 non-skippable per CLAUDE.md. Must produce ux-spec.md before any view code. Mapping confirmation UI needs careful UX design (auto-accept + manual review for ambiguous cases).

transitions.note: New feature added to backlog. Scope: import training plans from external sources (Hevy, Strong, Fitbod, Jefit, spreadsheets, PDFs, photos, share extension, paste-to-parse). Research phase pending — awaiting prioritization.

source_investigation_areas: CSV/JSON import — simplest format, used by Strong/Hevy export
PDF text extraction — reuse OCR from nutrition feature
Photo OCR — handwritten/printed whiteboard or printouts
Share extension — iOS native app-to-app sharing
Exercise name mapping — ML or rule-based, 87-exercise target library
Progression rules — linear, wave, percentage-based, RPE-based
Program structure — day splits, phases, deload weeks

success_criteria_draft.target: >80% of imports produce a usable training plan without manual fixes
kill_criteria: Parse time >30s for any supported format
